fourthSection.description:
Landmark Headquarters is a purpose-built corporate development in Dubai, designed to represent the identity, scale, and operational needs of the Landmark Group. The project was developed as a modern office headquarters with strong emphasis on functionality, efficiency, and environmental performance.

The building is a LEED Silver–compliant development, reflecting a commitment to sustainable design and responsible construction practices. Its architectural composition and internal planning are aligned with corporate requirements, supporting efficient workplace layouts and long-term operational flexibility.

SP International executed the project under a lump sum contract model, delivering the construction of an iconic structure comprising 3 basement levels, a ground floor, 7 dedicated parking floors, and 19 office floors. The headquarters is equipped with 12 high-speed elevators to support smooth internal movement and operational efficiency for a large workforce. Disciplined execution, quality control, and coordination across all trades enabled the successful completion of this landmark corporate facility, contributing to Dubai’s portfolio of premium commercial developments. The project reflects SP International’s capability to deliver large-scale, sustainability-compliant corporate buildings with precision.

fourthSection.title: More Details
fourthSection.description:
Lanai Island Villas forms part of the exclusive Tilal Al Ghaf master development, offering a highly curated collection of 27 ultra-luxury mansions designed for refined, resort-style living. Set on a private island, the villas are positioned to maximise views of the crystal lagoon, with each residence featuring expansive indoor–outdoor spaces, landscaped courtyards, and private spa pools that create a serene and immersive living experience.

The development blends contemporary architecture with natural surroundings, emphasising privacy, tranquillity, and comfort. With spacious seven- and eight-bedroom layouts, the mansions offer a rare balance of sophistication and seclusion while remaining connected to one of Dubai’s most sought-after residential destinations. The island’s layout, pedestrian-friendly pathways, and integrated landscape design contribute to a harmonious and elegant environment.

SP International’s scope covers the full spectrum of construction for all 27 luxury units, including substructure and superstructure execution, advanced waterproofing systems, aluminium and glazing works, and the delivery of high-quality façades. The team is also responsible for MEP installations beneath slabs, boundary wall construction, and both in-plot and public realm landscaping. Deep and shallow infrastructure works form an essential part of the overall development, ensuring seamless connectivity and high-performance utilities across the island.

Executing a project of this calibre requires exceptional coordination, stringent quality control, and a thorough understanding of high-end residential expectations. SP International’s disciplined planning, precision engineering, and collaborative execution approach ensure that each mansion meets the highest standards of craftsmanship and

performance. Through its commitment to excellence, SP International supports Majid Al Futtaim’s vision of establishing Lanai Island as one of Dubai’s most distinguished luxury enclaves.

items.value: M/s Indoocean Developers (Pvt.) Ltd, Sri Lanka

fourthSection.title: More Details
fourthSection.description:
Altair is one of the most recognisable high-rise developments in Sri Lanka, comprising 68 storeys and rising approximately 240 metres, positioning it among the tallest structures in the country. The development delivers over 400 luxury residential apartments, designed to offer elevated urban living with uninterrupted views across Colombo and the surrounding coastline. 

A defining feature of the project is the Sky Garden located on the 63rd floor, which includes a swimming pool, resident lounge, and dedicated entertainment areas. This shared amenity level enhances community living within a vertical environment while reinforcing the project’s focus on lifestyle, comfort, and exclusivity. 

The development consists of two interconnected towers, one of which is visually perceived as leaning against the other, creating a distinctive architectural profile on Colombo’s skyline. This unique form is supported by an advanced diagrid structural system combined with flat slab construction, enabling high structural efficiency while eliminating the need for internal columns within apartments. As a result, residential units benefit from flexible layouts and expansive internal spaces. 

The scope of works included the construction and completion of both towers, incorporating complex structural coordination, façade execution, and high-rise construction logistics. The permeable façade design allows for large window openings, maximising daylight penetration and panoramic views while maintaining structural integrity. The successful delivery of Altair demonstrates SPINT’s capability in executing architecturally ambitious, technically complex, and large-scale residential towers within dense urban environments.

This project represents the pilot phase of a major mass housing initiative in Ghana, involving the construction of 240 residential units out of a planned total of 4,000 apartments. Developed by M/s Sethi Realty, the project was conceived as a benchmark development to support the implementation of affordable housing programmes across the country. 

The development is recognised as the first four-storey RCC framed affordable housing project of its kind in Ghana. As an initial phase, it was designed to validate construction methodologies, structural systems, and execution capability before rolling out the remaining housing units under the larger master plan. 

Shapoorji Pallonji was engaged as the main contractor and delivered the project amidst significant logistical, technical, and operational challenges. Through detailed planning, disciplined execution, and consistent site coordination, the project progressed to completion despite constraints. The successful delivery reinforced SPINT’s commitment, reliability, and assurance in executing affordable housing developments under demanding conditions, establishing the project as a reference model within the Ghanaian housing sector. 

The completed development comprises four blocks of two-bedroom apartments and four blocks of three-bedroom apartments, all constructed with reinforced concrete frames. The housing blocks are supported by open roof terraces, a centralised drainage and water supply system, well-planned internal road networks, and ample parking facilities. These integrated infrastructure provisions ensure functional efficiency, ease of maintenance, and improved living standards for residents, contributing to the long-term sustainability of the housing community.

firstSection.title: NAWEC Water Project, Gambia
firstSection.subTitle: National water infrastructure upgrade supporting safe and reliable supply

fourthSection.title: More Details
fourthSection.description:
The NAWEC Water Project is a priority national infrastructure initiative aimed at strengthening water supply capacity across The Gambia. Implemented under the Line of Credit facility from the Government of India, the project supports the country’s efforts to provide consistent and adequate potable water to its population. 

The development has been undertaken to replace ageing water infrastructure, including asbestos-based pipelines that have been in service for more than 70 years and declared hazardous by the World Health Organization (WHO). The project focuses on improving water quality, public health standards and long-term system reliability. 

The scope of works includes the execution of 8 new borewells and the construction of new water reservoirs with a capacity of 1.5 million litres. It also involves the laying of approximately 34 kilometres of HDPE water transmission pipelines and 281 kilometres of uPVC water distribution pipelines, including the systematic replacement of existing asbestos pipelines with safer uPVC systems. These interventions are designed to improve hydraulic efficiency, reduce leakage losses and enhance distribution coverage. 

In addition to the core water supply works, the project incorporates associated electrical and mechanical systems essential for operational continuity. This includes the laying of 13.2 kilometres of 11 KVA electrical transmission lines, replacement of old pumps at existing water stations, and full integration of the new borewells into the national water network. Collectively, these works establish a modern, resilient and sustainable water supply system aligned with The Gambia’s long-term infrastructure development goals.

The development includes a multi-storey commercial building, a separate chiller plant facility, and comprehensive external works. A significant component of the project involved extensive external works to integrate the development seamlessly with its surroundings. These works included retaining walls, storm water drainage systems, external services, holding tanks, kerbstone installation, and interlock paving, all executed to ensure site stability, effective drainage, and safe circulation. The scope of works covered structural construction using RCC and steel structures, architectural finishes, and complete MEP installations for the main building. The chiller plant houses chillers, transformers, cooling towers, water tanks, and pump sets, forming a centralised cooling and utility backbone for the complex. The project was delivered with a focus on construction quality, service integration, and durability, resulting in a well-balanced commercial development with dependable infrastructure support.

firstSection.title: New Mooring Facility at Shannah
firstSection.subTitle: Offshore and onshore maritime infrastructure supporting coastal operations

fourthSection.title: More Details
fourthSection.description:
The New Mooring Facility at Shannah is a strategically developed marine infrastructure project combining offshore and onshore works to support coastal operations and maritime activities. The project was planned to enhance berthing capability, operational safety, and supporting utilities within the Shannah coastal zone. 

Offshore works formed a major component of the development and included the construction of an approach trestle, quay wall, ROP patrol berth, landing area, and an island breakwater. These elements were designed to accommodate marine vessels safely while ensuring durability against challenging marine and environmental conditions. The offshore structures were carefully sequenced to maintain stability and alignment during construction. 

Onshore works complemented the marine infrastructure and comprised the construction of support buildings such as a mosque, utility building, and guard rooms, along with associated infrastructure. These works also included provision of water supply, sewerage systems, electrical services, and drainage networks, ensuring the facility could operate independently and efficiently. 

The approach trestle, quay wall, and ROP patrol berth were founded on RCC vertical bored cast-in-situ piles, providing the necessary load-bearing capacity and resistance to marine forces. The island breakwater was constructed using quarry stones of varying sizes, with a protective top layer of core-loc armour units to enhance wave energy dissipation. firstSection.title: DEWA Water Transmission Pipelines Project, Dubai
firstSection.subTitle: High-capacity water transmission infrastructure strengthening Dubai’s supply network

secondSection.project: DEWA – Supply, Installation, Testing and Commissioning of GRE Water

fourthSection.title: More Details
fourthSection.description:
The DEWA Water Transmission Pipelines Project is a large-scale infrastructure initiative aimed at strengthening Dubai’s potable water transmission network across multiple strategic locations within the emirate. The project involves the supply, installation, testing, and commissioning of high-diameter Glass Reinforced Epoxy (GRE) pipelines, forming a critical backbone for long-term water security and operational resilience. 

The works are executed across multiple pipeline corridors, each requiring careful coordination within dense urban environments and live utility zones. The project’s extended duration reflects its scale, phased delivery approach, and the complexity of integrating new transmission assets with DEWA’s existing water infrastructure while maintaining uninterrupted service to end users. 

Shapoorji Pallonji Mideast LLC is delivering the project through disciplined execution, stringent quality control, and phased commissioning strategies. Lines 01, 02, 03, and 06—covering a total length of 22.72 km—have been successfully commissioned and have received Taking Over Certificates (TOC). Line 04 is currently under commissioning over a 6.80 km stretch, with completion targeted by January 2026. Each phase is executed in compliance with DEWA standards, safety requirements, and operational benchmarks. 

The remaining scope under Line 05 has progressed through design and trial trench stages, with DEWA Design NOC approvals secured and Construction NOC approvals under process. Upon approval, pipelaying works will commence immediately. With planned progress at 87.51% and actual progress closely aligned at 87.39%, the project demonstrates strong schedule control, financial discipline, and execution consistency, reinforcing SPINT’s capability in delivering complex, high-value water infrastructure projects for critical public utilities.
